title: Bloom Energy Stock Analysis After Oracle Deal
text: Bloom Energy shares surged 25.7% following announcement of an expanded partnership with Oracle. The company will provide up to 2.8 GW of fuel cell capacity to power Oracle's Project Jupiter AI data center campus in New Mexico. Q1 2026 results showed revenue of $751.05 million, net income of $70.65 million, and full-year 2026 guidance raised to $3.40-$3.80 billion. The investment narrative frames Bloom's thesis around solid oxide fuel cells staying central to solving AI data center power constraints while managing exposure to natural gas dependency and manufacturing capital intensity. The analysis projects a fair value of $111.18 per share, suggesting potential 62% downside from current levels. Bloom's dependence on natural gas feedstock leaves the business exposed to competitive and regulatory pressures as renewable energy and storage technologies advance more rapidly than anticipated.
